artifacts.summary: Detailed analysis of blocking issue in multi-family W/D separation approach

partial_progress.details: Phase 1 blocked due to architectural gap between temporally_coherent definition and multi-family approach

review_reason: Phase 1 blocked due to fundamental architectural gap. The temporally_coherent definition requires per-family F/P witnesses, but the multi-family approach addresses modal (not temporal) coherence. Canonical witnesses from canonicalMCS_forward_F are in CanonicalMCS domain, not at Rat times. See implementation summary for detailed analysis and possible resolution paths.

completion_data.summary: Implementation blocked during Phase 1 analysis. The multi-family W/D separation approach from the plan has an architectural gap: temporally_coherent is a per-family requirement, but witnesses from canonicalMCS_forward_F are in CanonicalMCS domain, not at Rat times. Multi-family addresses modal coherence, not the needed temporal coherence. No code written; analysis documented in implementation summary.
blocking_issues: temporally_coherent requires per-family F/P witnesses at domain times
canonicalMCS_forward_F witnesses are in CanonicalMCS, not assignable to Rat times
Multi-family BFMCS addresses modal coherence (Box/Diamond), not temporal coherence (G/H/F/P)
Constant witness families fail forward_F (F(phi) in W does not imply phi in W)
possible_resolutions: Modify temporally_coherent to bundle-level (requires truth lemma rewrite)
Prove CanonicalMCS completeness, derive dense completeness semantically
Fix TimelineQuot forward_F via dovetailing (22+ hour path from Task 982)
Alternative semantic proof restructuring truth lemma F/P cases
